01 What’s New in v3

// Three new modules, 7 new UI pages, full distributed layer

v3 adds the complete peer communication and distributed storage layer on top of the v2 core engine. The existing block/DAG/events/simulator/attack modules are unchanged. Everything new is additive:

Areav2v3 (new)
HSM signingSingle epoch key (shared)Per-block derived key via HKDF; epoch rotation every 10 blocks
PeersNone — single processFull mesh of 3 peers, each with own HSM + DAG + store
CommunicationNoneHMAC-signed message envelopes; TASK, BLOCK, TELEMETRY, VALIDATE_REQ
StorageNonePluggable BaseStore; MemoryStore (default) + FileStore (swap-in)
ValidationHub-local onlyCross-peer validation with consensus percentage + epoch token proof
UI pages714 (added: Peer Mesh, Task Dispatch, Cross-Validate, Storage, Telemetry, HSM/Epochs)
API endpoints1022 (added: /peers/*, /storage/*, /hsm/status)
The header bar now shows five live indicators: ONLINE status, block count, event count, current epoch, and peer count. All five auto-refresh every 5 seconds.

02 System Architecture

// How all modules connect in v3

Full-Stack Map

Web UI (static/index.html)
Dashboard DAG Graph Execution Pipeline Peer Mesh Task Dispatch Cross-Validate Storage Telemetry Inspector Blocks HSM/Epochs Attack Sim
FastAPI Server (api/server.py) — 22 endpoints
DAG/Block/Pipeline endpoints Peer mesh endpoints Storage endpoints HSM status endpoint
Hub + HSM v2 (hub/controller.py + hsm/demo_hsm.py)
HKDF epoch key → Per-block derived key → HMAC-SHA256 signature → Block in hub DAG
Peer Mesh (peer/mesh.py + peer/node.py)
peer-alpha HSM | DAG | Store | Inbox    peer-beta    peer-gamma
All wired bidirectionally — each pair can TASK, BLOCK, TELEMETRY, VALIDATE
Storage Layer (storage/store.py)
MemoryStore (default) ↔ FileStore (swap-in) ↔ RedisStore (add your own)

Project Structure

warlok/
  ├─ core/— block, dag, events, simulator, attack (unchanged from v2)
  ├─ hsm/
  │   └─ demo_hsm.py— v3: HKDF per-block keys + epoch rotation
  ├─ hub/
  │   └─ controller.py— v3: epoch-stamped blocks + epoch-aware validate
  ├─ peer/— NEW in v3
  │   ├─ node.py— PeerNode: HSM + DAG + Store + message handlers
  │   └─ mesh.py— Mesh coordinator: dispatch, replicate, telemetry, cross-validate
  ├─ storage/— NEW in v3
  │   └─ store.py— BaseStore, MemoryStore, FileStore, StorageRecord, StorageReceipt
  ├─ api/
  │   └─ server.py— v3: 22 endpoints, mesh + storage + hsm status
  ├─ static/
  │   └─ index.html— v3: 14 pages, epoch badges, peer cards, consensus ring
  └─ requirements.txt— fastapi, uvicorn[standard], requests (no new deps)

New API Endpoints

MethodPathDescription
GET/healthNow returns hsm status dict + peers list
GET/hsm/statusNEWepoch_id, block_count, epoch_size, next_rotation_in, cached_keys
GET/peersNEWFull mesh status: all peer nodes with DAG, store, inbox, HSM stats
GET/peers/{peer_id}NEWSingle peer full status
POST/peers/taskNEWDispatch signed task from one peer to another; returns block_hash + receipt
POST/peers/replicateNEWPush a block from source peer to target peers
POST/peers/telemetryNEWRoute raw data bytes from one peer to another’s store
POST/peers/cross_validateNEWAsk all peers to validate a hash; returns consensus % + per-peer results
GET/storage/hubNEWHub store stats
POST/storage/hub/putNEWManual put to hub store
GET/storage/hub/get/{key}NEWRetrieve record from hub store with checksum verification
GET/storage/{peer_id}NEWPeer store stats (records, bytes)
GET/storage/{peer_id}/get/{key}NEWRetrieve record from a peer’s store

03 HSM v2 — Per-Block Key Derivation & Epoch Rotation

// hsm/demo_hsm.py — HKDF-based key hierarchy

v2 used a single shared epoch key for all signing. v3 derives a unique key for every block using HKDF, and rotates the epoch key every 10 blocks. This means compromising one block’s key reveals nothing about any other block’s signature.

HKDF Key Hierarchy

root_key = b"warlok-root-secret"
└— epoch_key = HKDF(root_key, "epoch:N") ← rotates every 10 blocks
└— block_key = HKDF(epoch_key, block_hash) ← unique per block
└— sig = HMAC-SHA256(block_key, hash_bytes)
hsm/demo_hsm.py — HKDF-lite derive() + per-block keyPython
def _derive(parent: bytes, context: bytes) -> bytes:
    # HKDF-lite: extract then expand with same context
    prk = hmac.new(parent, context, hashlib.sha256).digest()
    return hmac.new(prk, context + b'', hashlib.sha256).digest()

class DemoHSM:
    def __init__(self, root_key):
        self.root_key  = root_key
        self._epoch_id = 0
        self._epoch_key = _derive(root_key, b"epoch:0")

    def _bkey(self, block_hash: str) -> bytes:
        # Derive unique key per block from current epoch key
        return _derive(self._epoch_key, block_hash.encode())

    def sign(self, data, block_hash=None):
        self._block_count += 1
        self._maybe_rotate()               # rotate if block_count // 10 changed
        key = self._bkey(block_hash) if block_hash else self._epoch_key
        return hmac.new(key, data, hashlib.sha256).digest()

    def verify_epoch(self, data, sig, block_hash, epoch_id):
        # Cross-epoch verify — re-derives from root, no live state needed
        ek = _derive(self.root_key, f"epoch:{epoch_id}".encode())
        bk = _derive(ek, block_hash.encode())
        return hmac.compare_digest(hmac.new(bk, data, ...).digest(), sig)

Epoch Token for Cross-Peer Validation

When peers cross-validate each other’s blocks, they need proof that the signing epoch was valid without exposing the raw epoch key. The epoch_token() method returns HMAC(epoch_key, b"peer-token") — a shareable proof that cannot be reversed to recover the key itself.

hsm/demo_hsm.py — epoch token (shareable, non-reversible)Python
def epoch_token(self, epoch_id=None) -> bytes:
    eid = epoch_id if epoch_id is not None else self._epoch_id
    ek  = _derive(self.root_key, f"epoch:{eid}".encode())
    # HMAC of epoch key — proves epoch knowledge without revealing key
    tok = hmac.new(ek, b"peer-token", hashlib.sha256).digest()
    return tok
The cross-validate UI shows the first 16 hex chars of each peer’s epoch token. All valid peers on the same epoch will produce the same token — this is the consensus proof.

04 Hub v2 — Epoch-Aware Signing & Validation

// hub/controller.py — blocks now carry epoch metadata

The Hub’s create_block() now signs with the per-block derived key and stamps the current epoch ID onto the block’s meta dict. This allows future validation of the block — even after epoch rotation — by re-deriving from the stored epoch ID.

hub/controller.py — epoch-stamped signingPython
def create_block(self, parents, payload, meta):
    b = Block(parents, payload, meta)
    h = b.compute_hash()
    # v3: per-block derived key, not epoch key
    b.signature = self.hsm.sign(h.encode(), block_hash=h)
    # v3: stamp epoch on block so validate() can re-derive the right key
    b.meta["_epoch"] = self.hsm.epoch_id
    self.dag.add(b)
    return b

def validate(self, h):
    b = self.dag.get(h)
    if not b: return False, "Missing"
    epoch = b.meta.get("_epoch")
    # Use epoch-aware re-derivation — works even after epoch has rotated
    ok = self.hsm.verify_epoch(b.hash.encode(), b.signature, b.hash, epoch)
    if not ok: return False, "Bad signature"
    for p in b.parents:      # recursive parent chain
        ok, msg = self.validate(p)
        if not ok: return False, msg
    return True, "VALID"
Every block in the Block Registry and DAG Graph now shows an E0 epoch badge, indicating which epoch key was used to sign it. After 10 blocks are signed, the next block shows E1, and so on.

05 Storage Layer — Pluggable Store Interface

// storage/store.py — BaseStore, MemoryStore, FileStore, StorageReceipt

BaseStore Contract

All storage backends must implement the same four methods. Callers (peers, hub, API endpoints) never interact with a concrete class — only with the BaseStore interface. Swapping MemoryStore for FileStore or RedisStore requires zero changes to calling code.

storage/store.py — BaseStore interfacePython
class BaseStore(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def put(self, key: str, value: bytes, meta: dict) -> StorageReceipt: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def get(self, key: str) -> StorageRecord | None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def list_keys(self, prefix: str = "") -> list[str]: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def delete(self, key: str) -> bool: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def stats(self) -> dict: ...

MemoryStore & FileStore

storage/store.py — MemoryStore.put() + integrity check on get()Python
class MemoryStore(BaseStore):
    backend_name = "memory"

    def put(self, key, value, meta=None):
        rec = StorageRecord(
            key=key, value=value, meta=meta or {},
            timestamp=time.time(),
            checksum=hashlib.sha256(value).hexdigest()  # stored for later integrity check
        )
        self._store[key] = rec
        return StorageReceipt(key=key, checksum=rec.checksum,
                               timestamp=rec.timestamp, backend="memory", ...)

    def get(self, key):
        rec = self._store.get(key)
        if rec and not rec.verify():     # re-hashes value, compares to stored checksum
            log_event("STORE_CORRUPT", ...)
            return None
        return rec

StorageReceipt

Every put() returns a StorageReceipt containing key, SHA-256 checksum, timestamp, backend name, and peer ID. This receipt is sent back through the message ACK chain so the original task dispatcher can verify the data actually landed and was checksummed correctly.

storage/store.py — StorageReceipt dataclassPython
@dataclass
class StorageReceipt:
    key:       str    # storage key
    checksum:  str    # SHA-256 hex of stored value
    timestamp: float  # unix epoch of storage operation
    backend:   str    # "memory" | "file" | "redis" | ...
    peer_id:   str    # which peer stored it
To swap to FileStore: replace MemoryStore(peer_id=...) with FileStore(root="/data/warlok", peer_id=...) in server.py. No other code changes required. For Redis or S3, implement BaseStore subclass.

06 Peer Node — HSM + DAG + Store + Inbox

// peer/node.py — each peer is a self-contained cryptographic agent

A PeerNode is an independent agent with its own HSM (sharing the root key with the hub, simulating shared hardware root-of-trust), its own local DAG for block replication, its own storage, and an inbox that records every received message.

Message Envelope & Transport Signing

Every message between peers is wrapped in a Message envelope and HMAC-signed at the transport layer — separate from the per-block HSM signing. This gives two independent layers of authentication: the message itself is authenticated (was it really from peer-alpha?), and each block within it carries its own HSM signature (is this block integrity-intact?).

peer/node.py — Message envelope + transport signingPython
@dataclass
class Message:
    msg_type:  str    # TASK | BLOCK | TELEMETRY | ACK | VALIDATE_REQ
    sender_id: str
    payload:   dict
    msg_id:    str    # uuid hex[:12] — unique per message
    timestamp: float
    signature: str    # hex HMAC of canonical payload

    def canonical(self) -> bytes:
        # Deterministic JSON of core fields — sorted keys
        return json.dumps({
            "msg_id": self.msg_id, "msg_type": self.msg_type,
            "sender_id": self.sender_id, "payload": self.payload,
            "timestamp": self.timestamp,
        }, sort_keys=True).encode()

# Transport-level signing (root key, not block key)
def _sign_msg(self, msg):
    raw = msg.canonical()
    sig = hmac.new(self.hsm.root_key, raw, hashlib.sha256).digest()
    return sig.hex()

Message Handlers

The peer’s receive() verifies the transport signature first, then dispatches to the appropriate handler. Each handler type creates a different kind of evidence trail:

TypeHandlerWhat it doesReturns in ACK
TASK _handle_task Runs task_runner(task), stores result, creates signed block block_hash, store_key, store_receipt, exec_status
BLOCK _handle_block Deserialises block, verify_epoch(), insert into local DAG + store replicated: bool, reason if failed
TELEMETRY _handle_telemetry Stores raw bytes under given key in local store stored: bool, receipt dict
VALIDATE_REQ _handle_validate_req Looks up block in local DAG, verify_epoch(), returns result + epoch token valid: bool, epoch, token hex, validator peer_id
peer/node.py — TASK handler (execution + block + storage)Python
def _handle_task(self, msg):
    task         = msg.payload.get("task", {})
    result_bytes = self.task_runner(task)   # execute the task
    store_key    = f"task:{msg.msg_id}"
    receipt      = self.store.put(store_key, result_bytes,   # store result
                                   meta={"task": task, "from": msg.sender_id})
    # Create a block recording this execution
    b = self._make_block(
        parents=msg.payload.get("parents", []),
        payload=json.dumps({"task": task, "store_key": store_key, ...}),
        meta={"agent": self.peer_id, "task_type": task.get("type")}
    )
    return {"block_hash": b.hash, "store_key": store_key,
            "store_receipt": receipt.to_dict(), "exec_status": "OK"}

07 Mesh Coordinator — High-Level Operations

// peer/mesh.py — wires peers + provides operation API

The Mesh class wires all PeerNode instances together bidirectionally on boot, then exposes four high-level operations that the FastAPI server calls. Each operation creates the right message type, routes it, logs the event, and returns a structured result.

peer/mesh.py — cross_validate with consensus scoringPython
def cross_validate(self, requestor_id, block_hash):
    requestor = self._nodes[requestor_id]
    results   = requestor.cross_validate(block_hash)  # sends VALIDATE_REQ to all peers

    valid_count   = sum(1 for r in results.values()
                        if r.get("status") == "OK" and r.get("valid"))
    consensus_pct = valid_count / len(results) * 100

    return {
        "block_hash":    block_hash,
        "valid_count":   valid_count,
        "total_peers":   len(results),
        "consensus_pct": round(consensus_pct, 1),
        "peer_results":  results,  # per-peer: valid, epoch, token
    }
In the demo, peers only replicate blocks that were explicitly sent via POST /peers/replicate. A production system would auto-gossip new blocks to all peers immediately after hub creation. The interface is already in place — mesh.replicate_block() — it just needs to be called from create_block().

08 UI Walkthrough — All 14 Pages

// static/index.html — single file, zero framework, 14 client-side pages

The UI is one HTML file (˜66KB) served by FastAPI. Auto-refreshes every 5 seconds. The header bar shows the live system state at a glance:

WARLOK | ● ONLINE | 5 blocks | 98 events | epoch:0 | 3 peers

Dashboard

Eight live stat tiles: Total Blocks, Active, Compromised, Events (top row) + Peers Online, HSM Epoch, Store Records, Next Rotation (bottom row). Quick create-block panel and inline validator. Live event stream — all component types.

Screen 1 — System Dashboard · 2 blocks · 25 events · 3 peers · epoch:0
8 stat tiles — top row: Total Blocks, Active, Compromised, Events. Bottom row: Peers Online (3, in purple), HSM Epoch (0), Store Records, Next Rotation (7 blocks until epoch key rotates). Live event stream shows PEER_INIT and PEER_REGISTER events from mesh boot, then HUB_CREATE and HSM_SIGN from block creation.

DAG Graph

HTML5 Canvas topology. Each node shows agent label, truncated hash, and an amber epoch badge in the top-right corner. Bezier edges with cyan gradient. Click any node to open the block modal with hash, agent, epoch, status, parents, and live validation.

Screen 2a — DAG Topology · Epoch badge top-right of each node
Screen 2b — Block modal · Hash, Agent, Epoch, Status, Parents, Validation
Left: The canvas shows 2 blocks connected by a bezier edge. Each node has a small amber E0 badge (epoch annotation). Right: Clicking a node opens the modal — showing full hash, agent, E0 epoch badge, ACTIVE status, genesis parents, and live ✓ VALID result.

Execution Log

Full reverse-chronological event stream from all components: hub, HSM, peers, mesh, storage. v3 adds 15 new event types visible here: PEER_SEND, PEER_RECV, PEER_TASK_EXEC, HSM_CROSS_VERIFY, STORE_PUT, MESH_TASK_DISPATCH, and more. Each has a distinct colour badge.

Screen 3 — Execution Log · All component events · v3 event types
Execution Log now shows 15 distinct event types from 5 components. Visible here: HSM_CROSS_VERIFY (amber, from a validate call), DAG_ADD / SIGNED, HSM_SIGN with epoch and mode:block, HUB_CREATE. The SIGNED event now carries an epoch field confirming which epoch key was used.

Pipeline Runner

Animated 4-agent runner unchanged from v2. The Pipeline Events panel now additionally shows SIGNED events with the epoch ID stamped on each block, confirming per-block key derivation.

Screen 4 — Pipeline Runner · All 4 agents complete · Epoch-stamped events
Pipeline Events panel shows PIPELINE_STEP, DAG_ADD, and SIGNED with epoch:0 for each agent block — confirming per-block HKDF key derivation is active. The animated flow diagram shows all 4 agent boxes green with their hash prefixes.

Peer Mesh

Three peer cards showing live state: DAG blocks, store records, inbox count, blocks originated, HSM epoch badge, known peers list, and an epoch progress bar. Below the cards: a mesh event stream filtered to peer/mesh/store events only.

Screen 5 — Peer Mesh · 3 peer cards · Boot event stream
Each peer card shows: DAG blocks (0 — no blocks replicated yet), Store records (0), Inbox (0), Originated (0), HSM epoch badge (E0), Peers known list, and an epoch progress bar at block 0/10. The Mesh Event Stream shows the full boot sequence: PEER_INIT for each peer, MESH_ADD_PEER with total count, and PEER_REGISTER for every bidirectional link (6 total for 3 peers).

Task Dispatch

Send a signed task from any peer to any other. Select: from peer, to peer, task type (compute / analyze / classify / echo / transform), task data, optional parent hash. The dispatch result panel shows the returned block hash, store key, and checksum — all from the executing peer’s ACK. The event log below filters to task/mesh events.

Screen 6a — Task Dispatch · First dispatch · block hash + checksum returned
Screen 6b — Task Dispatch · Second dispatch · Different block hash per task
Left (first dispatch): Status OK, Exec OK, Block hash d057a5... (peer-beta's own block), Store key task:2867ca78f0d1, Checksum 6ffd6aa6b.... Right (second dispatch): New block hash eb81bbc87b..., new store key task:2424358b633d, different checksum — each task creates an independent block and storage record on the executing peer.

Cross-Validate

Select a requestor peer and paste a block hash. The requestor sends VALIDATE_REQ messages to all other peers. Results are aggregated into a consensus ring (percentage), and a table shows each peer’s independent verdict: VALID/INVALID, epoch badge, and the first 16 chars of their epoch token.

The screenshot below shows 0% NO CONSENSUS because the block being validated only exists in the hub’s DAG — it has not been replicated to the peers yet. This is the expected result when cross-validating a hub block without first running POST /peers/replicate.

Screen 7a — Cross-Validate · Empty (before running)
Screen 7b — Cross-Validate · 0% NO CONSENSUS · Peers report INVALID
Left: Clean state with placeholder input. Right: The consensus ring shows 0% in red, NO CONSENSUS, 0/2 peers confirmed valid. Per-peer table: peer-beta and peer-gamma both report INVALID with E0 epoch badges and Status OK (they responded successfully, but the block is not in their local DAG — it was never replicated from the hub). This is the expected result and confirms the replication boundary.

Storage Monitor

Four stat tiles showing record count and byte total per storage node (hub, peer-alpha, peer-beta, peer-gamma). Manual hub store put/get panel. Retrieve panel for any peer’s store with checksum verification display. Storage events log filtered to STORE_PUT, STORE_GET, STORE_CORRUPT.

Screen 8 — Storage Monitor · Hub:1 record · peer-beta:2 records
Four stat tiles: hub (1 record, 10 bytes — from manual put), peer-alpha (0), peer-beta (2 records, 120 bytes — from two task dispatches), peer-gamma (0). The Storage Events log shows three STORE_PUT events: manual-key to hub, and two task result stores to peer-beta (64 bytes each — JSON-encoded task results).

Telemetry Routing

Push raw data bytes peer-to-peer. Select from/to peer, storage key, and data payload. The receipt panel on the right shows the returned StorageReceipt: key, backend (memory), peer ID in purple, checksum, and STORED status. The Telemetry Events log shows MESH_TELEMETRY, PEER_TELEMETRY, and STORE_PUT events.

Screen 9 — Telemetry Routing · peer-alpha → peer-beta · Receipt shown
After SEND TELEMETRY: the Storage Receipt panel shows Key=sensor:demo, Backend=memory, Peer=peer-beta (purple), Checksum=83bb42bc49508a6ae274... (first 20 chars), Status=STORED. The Telemetry Events log shows the full chain: MESH_TELEMETRY, PEER_TELEMETRY, then STORE_PUT events including the earlier manual and task stores.

Block Inspector

Hash lookup with separate INSPECT (metadata) and VALIDATE (recursive chain validation) actions. v3 adds the Epoch row showing the amber E0 badge for every block. The validation panel shows the large checkmark and VALID label — or the failure reason including “Bad signature” for tampered blocks.

Screen 10a — Block Inspector · After INSPECT only
Screen 10b — Block Inspector · After VALIDATE · ✓ VALID
Left: After clicking INSPECT: Block Metadata panel shows full hash (cyan), Agent=—, Epoch=E0 (amber badge), Status=ACTIVE, Parents=(genesis). Validation panel is empty. Right: After clicking VALIDATE: large green checkmark and VALID label appear in the right panel — the recursive verify_epoch() call succeeded.

Block Registry

Full DAG table. v3 adds an Epoch column with amber badge per block. Three action buttons per row: INSPECT (navigate to Inspector), TARGET (send to Attack Sim), and X-VAL (navigate to Cross-Validate with hash pre-filled).

Screen 11 — Block Registry · 6 blocks · Epoch column · X-VAL button
v3 adds the Epoch column (amber E0 badge per block) and a third action button X-VAL that pre-fills the Cross-Validate hash field and navigates to that page. Two blocks show agent=— (genesis/manual blocks), four show Collector/Analyzer/Decision/Executor from the pipeline run.

HSM / Epochs Monitor

Three tiles: Current Epoch, Blocks Signed, Next Rotation In (blocks remaining until epoch key rotates). Epoch progress bar with block count. Interactive key derivation hierarchy diagram showing the live epoch number. HSM events log filtered to all HSM_* types: HSM_SIGN (purple), HSM_CROSS_VERIFY (amber), with data payloads showing block hash prefix and epoch number.

Screen 12a — HSM/Epochs Monitor · 9 blocks signed · 1 until rotation
Screen 12b — HSM/Epochs · Event log · HSM_SIGN + HSM_CROSS_VERIFY events
Left: Current Epoch=0, Blocks Signed=9, Next Rotation In=1. The epoch progress bar is nearly full (9/10). Key derivation hierarchy shows epoch_key = HKDF(root_key, "epoch:0") live. Right: HSM Events log: HSM_CROSS_VERIFY events (amber) from validate calls, then repeated HSM_SIGN events (purple) from block creation — each with epoch:0 and mode:block, confirming per-block key derivation is active.

Attack Simulator

Unchanged payload tamper and signature break primitives. The warning banner in v3 now adds: “All actions are logged and detectable via cross-validation.” The target list now shows both INVALID status badge and E0 epoch badge per block. After an attack, cross-validate the compromised hash to see 0% consensus.

Screen 13 — Attack Simulator · Tamper executed · Toast notification
After TAMPER PAYLOAD on the genesis block: the target list shows INVALID (red) + E0 (amber) badges. One ATTACK event in the Attack Log. The toast bottom-right reads "Tampered: dd4e5e836ccbb9a...". Warning banner now includes: "All actions are logged and detectable via cross-validation." — cross-validating this hash will now return 0% consensus from all peers.

09 Running the System

// Install, start, first-run sequence
TerminalShell
pip install -r requirements.txt   # fastapi, uvicorn[standard], requests

# Start from warlok/ directory (server.py in api/)
uvicorn api.server:app --reload --port 8000

# Web UI   →  http://localhost:8000
# API docs →  http://localhost:8000/docs
On boot, the server automatically creates three PeerNode instances (peer-alpha, peer-beta, peer-gamma) and wires them all bidirectionally via Mesh.add_peer(). The PEER_INIT and PEER_REGISTER events are visible in the Dashboard event stream immediately.

Recommended First-Run Sequence

1
Dashboard → RUN PIPELINECreates 5 epoch-stamped blocks; watch HUB_CREATE, HSM_SIGN, SIGNED events stream in
2
Peer Mesh → REFRESHSee all 3 peer cards with epoch progress bars at block 0 / 10
3
Task Dispatch → DISPATCH TASKpeer-alpha → peer-beta; see block hash + store receipt in result panel
4
Telemetry → SEND TELEMETRYpush raw data to peer-beta; confirm receipt with checksum
5
Storage → REFRESHpeer-beta now shows 2+ records; retrieve them with the GET panel
6
Block Registry → X-VAL on any blockcross-validate navigates to Cross-Validate with hash pre-filled
7
Cross-Validate → CROSS-VALIDATEHub blocks not replicated to peers: expect 0% consensus (correct — blocks not yet replicated)
8
Attack Sim → TAMPER a block → Cross-Validate itConfirm 0% consensus: tampered block fails HSM.verify_epoch() on every peer
9
HSM/Epochs → run 10+ more pipeline blocksWatch epoch rotate to E1; see HSM_EPOCH_ROTATE event flash amber in execution log

10 End-to-End Flow: Task → Block → Store → Cross-Validate

// The complete path from task dispatch to consensus proof
→
UI: POST /peers/task{"from":"peer-alpha","to":"peer-beta","task":{"type":"compute",...}}
→
mesh.dispatch_task()Creates signed Message envelope: HMAC(root_key, canonical_json) = transport sig
→
peer-alpha.send("peer-beta", "TASK", payload)PEER_SEND event logged; peer-beta.receive() called in-process
→
peer-beta._verify_msg()Checks transport HMAC; AUTH_FAIL if any byte differs
→
peer-beta._handle_task()Runs task_runner(task) → result_bytes
→
peer-beta.store.put("task:<msg_id>", result_bytes)STORE_PUT event; SHA-256 checksum computed; StorageReceipt created
→
peer-beta._make_block(parents, payload, meta)Block hashed, signed with peer-beta’s HKDF-derived block_key, inserted into peer-beta’s DAG
→
ACK returnsblock_hash, store_key, store_receipt (checksum + backend + peer_id), exec_status
→
POST /peers/cross_validaterequestor sends VALIDATE_REQ to peer-beta and peer-gamma; both call verify_epoch()
→
Consensus computedvalid_count / total_peers * 100 = consensus%; epoch tokens compared for proof
The two-layer security model in one sentence: The transport layer (HMAC of message envelope) proves who sent the message. The block layer (HKDF-derived per-block HMAC) proves what was signed and when. Cross-validation uses the block layer only — peers re-derive the block key from their own root and the stored epoch ID, independently confirming the signature without any live communication with the signing node.
WARLOK
Pilot Console v3.0 — Developer Tutorial — March 2026
FastAPI · HKDF Epoch Keys · Full Peer Mesh · Pluggable Storage · Canvas DAG · 14-Page Dark UI
